preamble: The diphthong /oi/ underwent a significant phonetic transformation in the history of Ancient Greek, evolving into the close front rounded vowel /y/. This change, primarily a process of monophthongization, simplified the two-vowel sequence into a single, distinct vowel sound.

examples.note: The diphthong <οι> in Koine Greek shifted to /y/, as evidenced by alternations with <υ> in vernacular texts and Latin script equivalents representing <οι> as <y>.

sources: The Old English Name of the S-Rune and “Sun” in Germanic | Journal of Germanic Linguistics | Cambridge Core.
Sophisticated Speakers. Atticistic Pronunciation in the Atticist Lexica [1 ed.] 3110440407, 9783110440409 - DOKUMEN.PUB.
